description: Close the remaining 16 real-failure clusters from ralph/s3-compat-90, lifting s3-tests pass-rate from 84.7% (150/177) to >=90% strict (target ~95%). Plus follow-up tests for the two P1 latent bugs surfaced by the prior cycle (koanf empty-env override + Cassandra null-version timeuuid sentinel) so the regression surface stays caught in CI. 7 stories.

userStories.title: Multipart copy edges (range parser + special chars + size handling)
userStories.description: As an S3 client copying multipart parts, the gateway must handle copy-source-range edges, special-character source URLs, and size-aware copy variants the same way AWS does.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/s3api/multipart_copy.go::uploadPartCopy` parses `x-amz-copy-source-range: bytes=start-end` rigorously: empty / malformed / `bytes=0-0` / out-of-bounds â†’ `InvalidRange`. Range over the whole object (`bytes=` omitted) is OK and copies the entire source.

Source URL is `x-amz-copy-source: /<src-bucket>/<src-key>`. The src-key segment is URL-decoded BEFORE bucket-name validation. Special chars (`+`, ` ` (space), `?`, `#`, non-ASCII) round-trip cleanly. Test fixture covers all four.
Multipart copy of variable-size sources (small + medium + large in one upload) returns the correct per-part ETag in the UploadPartCopyResult XML â€” not the whole-source ETag.
s3-tests `test_multipart_copy_small`, `test_multipart_copy_improper_range`, `test_multipart_copy_special_names`, `test_multipart_copy_multiple_sizes` pass.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: parseCopySourceRangeStrict differentiates parse errors (InvalidArgument 400) from out-of-bounds (InvalidRange 416). parseCopySource splits on `?` BEFORE PathUnescape so literal `?` in keys (sent as `%3F` by boto3) does not get treated as query separator. Per-part ETag wire shape already correct in copyPartResult.

userStories.title: ?partNumber=N GET quoted-ETag wire shape
userStories.description: As an S3 client comparing a multipart object's per-part ETag from `?partNumber=N` against the part-upload's response ETag, the wire shape must match the upstream s3-tests' assertion (double-quoted hex, no inner stripping).
acceptanceCriteria: Audit `internal/s3api/server.go::getObject` partNumber path: response `ETag` header for the per-part response MUST be `"<32hex>"` (literal double quotes around hex), matching the part-upload's response format. Today the path may strip / re-quote inconsistently.
SSE-C variant of `?partNumber=N` GET returns the same quoted shape (the SSE wrapping must not double-quote or strip).
Single-part-object `?partNumber=1` GET returns the whole-object ETag's per-part-shape (still `"<32hex>"`).
s3-tests `test_multipart_get_part`, `test_multipart_sse_c_get_part`, `test_multipart_single_get_part` pass.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Surprise: PRD AC #1 said per-part GET should return per-part `"<32hex>"` shape. AWS-parity is the OPPOSITE â€” `?partNumber=N` GET returns the WHOLE-OBJECT multipart ETag (`"<32hex>-<count>"`). The s3-tests assert `response['ETag'] == complete_multipart_upload['ETag']`. Removed the partRange.ETag override in `internal/s3api/server.go::getObject`. Also flipped out-of-range partNumber from 416 InvalidRange to 400 InvalidPart (AWS-parity). Non-multipart partNumber=1 now returns whole object (200 OK).

userStories.title: Multipart composite checksum CRC32 / CRC32C / CRC64NVME
userStories.description: As an S3 client using boto3 â‰Ą1.35's auto-checksum (which ships `crc64nvme` as the default), `CompleteMultipartUpload` must return the correct composite checksum for CRC algos. SHA1/SHA256 closed in prior US-003; CRC family is still off.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/s3api/multipart.go::completeMultipartUpload` computes composite CRC32 / CRC32C / CRC64NVME over per-part raw checksum bytes. Algo dispatch via the existing `data.Manifest.PartChunks[N].ChecksumAlgorithm` field.

Helper `internal/auth` or `internal/data` (decide at story start) gets `FoldCRC32`, `FoldCRC32C`, `FoldCRC64NVME` reusable functions matching AWS's composite algorithm (may be concat-then-CRC; verify against AWS docs at story start).
Response XML includes the correct `<ChecksumCRC32>` / `<ChecksumCRC32C>` / `<ChecksumCRC64NVME>` element (whichever algo was set on Initiate).
HEAD object with `x-amz-checksum-mode: ENABLED` returns the composite CRC header.
s3-tests `test_multipart_use_cksum_helper_crc32`, `_crc32c`, `_crc64nvme` pass.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Surprise vs PRD wording (concat-then-CRC): the s3-tests pass `checksum_type="FULL_OBJECT"` for CRC32/CRC32C/CRC64NVME. AWS's FULL_OBJECT shape is the CRC of the concatenated stream (no `-N` suffix), composed via standard zlib `crc32_combine` from per-part (CRC, size). SHA family stays COMPOSITE. `composeMultipartChecksums(parts, checksumType)` dispatches; `combineCRCParts` lives in `internal/s3api/checksum.go` along with reusable `crc32Combine` / `crc64Combine` helpers. `checksumPart` grew a `Size int64` field. Locked s3-tests vectors via `TestFullObjectCRCS3TestVectors` (per-part CRCs + 5 MiB sizes â†’ published composite) plus a HTTP round-trip `TestMultipartFullObjectCRCCombine` for all 3 algos.

userStories.title: Multipart If-Match-on-missing-object: 404 NoSuchKey (revert RFC alignment for missing-object branch)
userStories.description: As an S3 client doing `If-Match` precondition on `CompleteMultipartUpload` against a non-existent destination object, AWS S3 returns 404 NoSuchKey rather than RFC 7232's 412 PreconditionFailed. Strata's prior US-008 aligned with RFC; the s3-tests assert AWS shape.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/s3api/multipart.go::completeMultipartUpload` precondition path: when `If-Match` is set AND the destination object does NOT exist, return `404 NoSuchKey` (NOT 412). The match-but-mismatched ETag path stays 412.
putObject's If-Match-missing-object path stays 412 (per RFC 7232 Â§3.1) â€” the divergence is multipart-Complete-only, AWS's exact shape.
s3-tests `test_multipart_put_object_if_match` and `test_multipart_put_current_object_if_match` pass.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Misc edge cases: suspended_copy / delete_key_bucket_gone / return_data_versioning / resend_finishes_last
userStories.description: As an S3 client, the gateway must handle four small edge cases identified in the prior cycle's baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: **`test_versioning_obj_suspended_copy`**: copy from a suspended-bucket null-row src to dst, response wire shape includes the SOURCE's `x-amz-version-id: null` header (or `<VersionId>null</VersionId>` in copy result XML).

**`test_object_delete_key_bucket_gone`**: DELETE on a non-existent bucket returns `404 NoSuchBucket` BEFORE the auth check rejects with 403. Need to swap the order in `internal/s3api/server.go` so the bucket-existence check runs ahead of the auth gate for DELETE.

**`test_bucket_list_return_data_versioning`**: V1 ListObjectVersions `<Owner>` field has `<DisplayName>` set to a non-empty value (today it's empty string). Wire `bucket.OwnerDisplayName` (or owner name from IAM) into the response.

**`test_multipart_resend_first_finishes_last`**: ROADMAP P2 entry â€” `meta.Store.CompleteMultipartUpload` flips upload status to `completing` BEFORE per-part ETag validation; a stale-ETag Complete leaks `completing` state and breaks idempotent retry. Fix: scan parts up front (status read-only) and only flip after every ETag matches. Lockstep across cassandra + memory + tikv impls.
All 4 s3-tests pass.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: P1 follow-up: koanf empty-env regression test + Cassandra null-sentinel integration test
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want CI tests covering the two P1 latent bugs the prior cycle surfaced so they cannot regress silently.
acceptanceCriteria: **koanf empty-env regression test** in `internal/config/config_test.go`: set TOML default `gc.interval="30s"`, set env `STRATA_GC_INTERVAL=""` (empty string), assert `cfg.GC.Interval == 30*time.Second`. Asserts the prior cycle's `env.ProviderWithValue` callback skips empty values correctly.

**Cassandra null-sentinel integration test** in `internal/meta/cassandra/store_integration_test.go` (build tag `integration`): PUT object into bucket with versioning=Suspended (so `o.IsNull=true && o.VersionID==NullVersionID`), GET it back, assert `gocql.ParseUUID` round-trip preserves `meta.NullVersionID` on read AND that the Cassandra row stores the v1 sentinel `00000000-0000-1000-8000-000000000000` (verify via raw SELECT).
Storetest contract `caseVersioningNullSentinel` extended to also exercise an INSERT with the null sentinel under a real backend (vs the existing in-memory shape).
CI workflow `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s `cassandra-integration` job ensures the new test runs on every Ralph commit (verify the test is NOT skipped â€” print test name in CI output).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass â€” including `make test-integration` against a real Cassandra container

userStories.title: Refresh s3-tests baseline + ROADMAP close-flip + cycle-end merge to main
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the latest s3-tests run captured in scripts/s3-tests/README.md, the ROADMAP P1 entry flipped to Done (this time we should hit >=90% strict), and the cycle merged back to main per branch policy.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `scripts/s3-tests/run.sh` against a clean `make up-all` stack (cassandra + ceph + strata, auth=required).

Append new section to `scripts/s3-tests/README.md` baseline list: `### YYYY-MM-DD - <commit-sha> + ralph/s3-compat-95 (N%)` with the pass-rate line + remaining failure breakdown (deliberate-gaps split + any real bugs left).
Verify pass rate >= 90% strict (target >= 95%). With the 16 real failures closed, expected: 166/177 = 93.8% strict.

If >= 90%: flip ROADMAP P1 entry `**P1 - s3-tests 80% -> 90%+.**` to `~~**P1 - s3-tests 80% -> 90%+.**~~ - **Done.** <one-line summary>. (commit \`<sha>\`)`. Update headline in ROADMAP.md introduction: `**84.7% (150/177)** -> **<new>% (<n>/177)**`.

Close (or fold into Done state) the four ROADMAP P2 entries created by the prior cycle: `Multipart copy edges`, `?partNumber=N quoted-ETag wire shape`, `Multipart composite checksum CRC32 / CRC32C / CRC64NVME`, `Multipart If-Match-on-missing-object error code`. Same close-flip format. Plus the resend-finishes-last P2.

**Cycle-end merge**: fast-forward / squash-merge `ralph/s3-compat-95` into `main`, push origin/main, archive `scripts/ralph/prd.json` + `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` under `scripts/ralph/archive/2026-MM-DD-s3-compat-95/`. Mirror the s3-compat-90 close shape.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
